text.title: Immersive Design Principles Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Immersive Design Principles methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Default comfort settings: snap turning enabled, vignette on, smooth-locomotion off-by-default. Smooth modes require opt-in.
rule.rationale: Default smooth-locomotion is the leading driver of new-user nausea.

rule.statement: Experiences MUST offer at least two locomotion options including teleport (or arm-swing) AS THE DEFAULT. Smooth-only locomotion is forbidden.

rationale.source: Microsoft MR comfort guidelines + IAAP XR

rule.rationale: Locomotion preference is highly individual; forced choice excludes users.

rule.statement: Audio peak limited to -3 dBFS; visual flashing limited per WCAG SC 2.3.1 (≤3/s, no ≥25% of viewport); haptic events ≤6/min unless user-extended.

rationale.source: WCAG SC 2.3.1 + Apple Vision sensory guidelines

rule.statement: Forced gaze direction OR forced action without affordance is forbidden. Every required user action carries a discoverable trigger.
rule.rationale: Forced action models exclude users with motor / cognitive differences.

rule.statement: A retreat-to-safe-scene (return-home / take-a-break) shortcut MUST be persistently available and reachable within 2 inputs.
rule.rationale: Users with anxiety / sensory overload need exit as a right.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent's context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-immersive-design-principles.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
